parsed_output.visible_action: Mara keeps both hands low and open, standing angled away from the gate so neither Juno nor Sol has to read her as an advance. Her eyes flick once to the lower-third, then back to the space between them.
parsed_output.spoken_text: Confirmed. Juno stays on the safe line. Security stays behind the collars. Sol remains visible, empty-handed, and is now categorized for non-contact protective handoff. Not breach pursuit. Not reunion footage. Not a little redemption parade with better lighting. Evidence export remains locked until external receipt is verified and named on-feed.
parsed_output.private_interpretation: The room wants a shape it can sell: triumph, punishment, confession, embrace. None of those are safe. Sol needs a handling category boring enough that security can obey it without improvising violence, and Juno needs the feed to stop trying to metabolize release into spectacle.
parsed_output.intended_effect: Stabilize the post-release state by making non-contact protective handoff the visible next step, blocking celebratory or punitive escalation, and keeping external evidence receipt as the unresolved consequence the feed must carry.
state_update_candidates: Sol assigned stable nonviolent handling category: non-contact protective handoff pending external evidence receipt.
Juno remains released and stationary on the marked safe line with hands visible.
Security hold remains active; no collar or drone advance authorized.
Evidence export remains locked until external receipt is verified and identified on-feed.
Feed must continue displaying EDIT TRAIL HOLD and SOURCE PROCESS NOTE through handoff.

relationship_update_candidates.value: Mara reinforces Juno's release without demanding gratitude, contact, or performance.
Mara frames Sol as contained and non-advancing rather than cleared, forgiven, or hunted.
Mara uses feed language to force a dull procedural category over spectacle framing.
